2603.21597.title: A Multidisciplinary AI Board for Multimodal Dementia Characterization and Risk Assessment
2603.21597.paper_summary: Modern clinical practice increasingly depends on reasoning over heterogeneous, evolving, and incomplete patient data. Although recent advances in multimodal foundation models have improved performance on various clinical tasks, most existing models remain static, opaque, and poorly aligned with real-world clinical workflows. We present Cerebra, an interactive multi-agent AI team that coordinates specialized agents for EHR, clinical notes, and medical imaging analysis. These outputs are synthesized into a clinician-facing dashboard that combines visual analytics with a conversational interface, enabling clinicians to interrogate predictions and contextualize risk at the point of care. Cerebra supports privacy-preserving deployment by operating on structured representations and remains robust when modalities are incomplete. We evaluated Cerebra using a massive multi-institutional dataset spanning 3 million patients from four independent healthcare systems. Cerebra consistently outperformed both state-of-the-art single-modality models and large multimodal language model baselines. In dementia risk prediction, it achieved AUROCs up to 0.80, compared with 0.74 for the strongest single-modality model and 0.68 for language model baselines. For dementia diagnosis, it achieved an AUROC of 0.86, and for survival prediction, a C-index of 0.81. In a reader study with experienced physicians, Cerebra significantly improved expert performance, increasing accuracy by 17.5 percentage points in prospective dementia risk estimation. These results demonstrate Cerebra's potential for interpretable, robust decision support in clinical care.
2603.21597.paper_summary_zh: 現代臨床實踐越來越依賴於對異質、演變中和不完整的患者數據進行推理。儘管最近在多模態基礎模型方面的進展改善了各種臨床任務的表現，但大多數現有模型仍然是靜態的、不透明的，並且與現實世界的臨床工作流程不佳對齊。我們提出了Cerebra，一個互動式多代理AI團隊，協調專門的代理進行電子健康紀錄（EHR）、臨床筆記和醫學影像分析。這些輸出被合成到一個面向臨床醫生的儀表板中，該儀表板結合了視覺分析和對話介面，使臨床醫生能夠詢問預測並在護理點上將風險情境化。Cerebra通過在結構化表示上運作來支持隱私保護的部署，並在模態不完整時保持穩健。我們使用一個涵蓋來自四個獨立醫療系統的300萬名患者的大型多機構數據集對Cerebra進行了評估。Cerebra在性能上始終超越了最先進的單模態模型和大型多模態語言模型基準。在癡呆風險預測中，它的AUROC達到了0.80，而最強的單模態模型為0.74，語言模型基準為0.68。在癡呆診斷中，它的AUROC達到了0.86，而在生存預測中，C-index為0.81。在一項針對經驗豐富的醫生的讀者研究中，Cerebra顯著提高了專家的表現，在前瞻性癡呆風險估計中提高了17.5個百分點的準確率。這些結果展示了Cerebra在臨床護理中可解釋的、穩健的決策支持潛力。
2603.21597.author: Sheng Liu et.al.
2603.21597.authors: Sheng Liu, Long Chen, Zeyun Zhao, Qinglin Gou, Qingyue Wei, Arjun Masurkar, Kevin M. Spiegler, Philip Kuball, Stefania C. Bray, Megan Bernath, Deanna R. Willis, Jiang Bian, Lei Xing, Eric Topol, Kyunghyun Cho, Yu Huang, Ruogu Fang, Narges Razavian, James Zou
